tooltips.Normal: Tile rule is not met. Mechanism is reading constructively or neutrally.
tooltips.Cautionary: Tile rule is partially met. Watch but do not act.
tooltips.Stressed: Tile rule is fully met. Mechanism is signaling its concerning regime.
tooltips.Distressed: Tile rule is fully met AND deteriorating over the last 60 trading days.

headline_gauge.headline_sentence: Two of 3 live cycle mechanisms are elevated above Normal — 1 in Stressed territory, 1 Cautionary.

tiles.name: Valuation
tiles.description_short: How richly equities are priced relative to history.
tiles.description_long: Valuation indicators measure how much investors are paying per dollar of earnings, GDP, or risk premium. When several measures simultaneously sit in the top quartile of their long-run distribution, the equity market is showing the cycle-peak signature — high prices, high prices for risk, narrow risk premium.

rule.description: Stressed when 3 of 4 indicators sit in their concerning quartile. For CAPE, P/E, and Buffett indicator the concerning quartile is the top 25% (rich valuation). For equity risk premium it is the bottom 25% (no compensation for owning stocks vs bonds). Cautionary when 2 of 4. Distressed when 3 of 4 AND worsening over 60 trading days.
rule.sprint_1_note: Sprint 1 ships with 3 of 4 indicators; trailing P/E joins in Sprint 1.5 once long-history Shiller series is staged.

indicators.name: CAPE (Shiller)
indicators.description: Cyclically-adjusted price-to-earnings ratio — S&P 500 price divided by 10-year average inflation-adjusted earnings. Smooths out earnings cycles so highs and lows are comparable across decades.
indicators.so_what: When CAPE sits in the top quartile of its long-run distribution, real subsequent 10-year returns have been low; when it sits in the bottom quartile, they have been high. Not a timing tool — a long-horizon valuation read.
indicators.formula: CAPE = S&P 500 price / (10-year average inflation-adjusted earnings per share)
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the top quartile of post-2011 (15y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: Equity Risk Premium
indicators.description: How much extra yield investors demand to own stocks vs 10-year Treasuries. Computed as earnings yield (1/CAPE) minus the 10-year yield. A near-zero or negative ERP means stocks are priced for perfection.
indicators.so_what: When ERP is at or below zero, investors are accepting less from stocks than they could get risk-free from Treasuries. Historically that compression has marked late-cycle equity tops.
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-2011 (15y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: Buffett Indicator
indicators.description: Nonfinancial corporate equity market cap as a percentage of GDP. Warren Buffett's preferred 'overall valuation' yardstick. Above 150% has historically preceded significant drawdowns; the post-1970 median is around 72%.
indicators.so_what: When this ratio sits at all-time-high levels — as it does today — the equity market is large relative to the economy backing it. Subsequent 10-year real returns have been low.
indicators.formula: Buffett = (Nonfinancial corp equity market cap / GDP) × 100
indicators.data_caveat: NCBCEL is nonfinancial corps only; the canonical Buffett indicator includes financial corps. Directional read is identical, level is slightly understated.
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the top quartile of post-1970 (~55y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

tiles.name: Credit
tiles.description_short: Compensation investors demand for default risk on corporate bonds.
tiles.description_long: Credit spreads describe how much extra yield bond investors require over risk-free Treasuries to take corporate-credit risk. The Credit tile reads as bidirectional: extreme tightness reads as cycle-peak complacency, extreme widening reads as actual stress arriving. Both are interesting in opposite ways.

rule.stressed_condition: 3 of 4 spreads in top quartile (real stress arriving)
rule.complacency_condition: 3 of 4 spreads in bottom quartile (priced for perfection)
rule.sprint_1_note: Sprint 1 ships with 3 of 4 indicators; leveraged-loan spread joins in Sprint 2.

indicators.name: IG OAS (Baa − 10y)
indicators.description: Yield premium investors require to own investment-grade Baa-rated corporate bonds over equal-maturity Treasuries. Widens in stress, compresses in complacency.
indicators.so_what: Today's IG OAS sits in the bottom quartile of post-1986 history. Investors are pricing very little default-risk premium into investment-grade credit — the 'priced for perfection' read.
indicators.formula: IG OAS proxy = Moody's Baa yield − 10-year Treasury yield, in basis points
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-1986 (~40y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: HY OAS
indicators.description: Yield premium on high-yield (junk-rated) corporate bonds over Treasuries. The most-watched single read on credit-market stress. Tight spreads = priced for perfection; wide spreads = stress is here.
indicators.so_what: At 284 bp HY OAS sits in the bottom quartile of the post-2011 sample. The high-yield market is pricing minimal default risk — a read consistent with late-cycle complacency rather than imminent stress.
indicators.formula: HY OAS = ICE BofA US High Yield Master II Option-Adjusted Spread, in basis points
indicators.data_caveat: ICE BofA license restricts FRED's free history to post-2011. GFC 2008 peak (~2,000bp) is out of sample.
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-2011 (15y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: HY/IG ratio
indicators.description: Ratio of high-yield spread to investment-grade spread. Strips out the level effect — captures whether credit markets are pricing the junk-vs-quality differential normally or in a stressed/complacent way.
indicators.so_what: Mid-distribution today. The junk-vs-quality differential is priced normally; nothing unusual either way in the relative pricing of high-yield to investment-grade.
indicators.formula: HY/IG ratio = HY OAS / IG OAS proxy (Baa − DGS10)
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the top quartile of post-2011 (15y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

tiles.name: Funding
tiles.description_short: Bank-system funding stress — SOFR-OIS, FRA-OIS, CDX IG-HY basis, cross-currency basis, CP funding spread.

tiles.name: Growth
tiles.description_short: How fast (or slow) the real economy is moving.
tiles.description_long: The Growth tile aggregates four real-economy reads: a broad activity index (CFNAI), an early labor-market signal (jobless claims), a manufacturing PMI proxy (ISM headline), and the bank-relative-equity signal (BKX vs S&P). The Growth rule fires only when indicators are BOTH at extreme levels AND deteriorating over 60 trading days — the 'AND' avoids permanently-elevated false alarms.

rule.description: Stressed when 3 of 4 indicators are both extreme (|z| > 1) AND deteriorating over 60 trading days. Cautionary when 2 of 4. Distressed when 3 of 4 AND deteriorating across all four.

indicators.name: CFNAI 3-month
indicators.description: Chicago Fed National Activity Index, 3-month moving average. A weighted average of 85 monthly indicators of US activity. Below −0.7 has historically signaled recession.
indicators.so_what: Mid-range today (z = +0.15) but trending down over the last 60 days. Not yet at the level-and-trend condition the rule requires; worth watching.
indicators.formula: 3-month moving average of the Chicago Fed National Activity Index (a weighted composite of 85 monthly US activity series)
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-2006 (20y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: Jobless claims (4w)
indicators.description: Initial unemployment claims, 4-week moving average. The earliest weekly read on labor-market deterioration.
indicators.so_what: At 214k, claims sit in the bottom quartile of the post-2006 sample. The labor market is tight; no early-warning signal here.
indicators.formula: 4-week moving average of seasonally-adjusted initial unemployment claims
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-2006 (20y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.description: Institute for Supply Management's composite manufacturing index. Below 50 = contraction, above = expansion. Used as a proxy for the new-orders subindex (paid feed). Caveat noted in methodology.
indicators.so_what: At 52.7 ISM is in expansion territory. Trend is mildly down over 60 days but still well above contraction (50).
indicators.formula: ISM Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index headline composite
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-2006 (20y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

indicators.name: Banks vs S&P 500 (BKX/SPX)
indicators.description: Ratio of the KBW Bank Index to the S&P 500. Banks lead the cycle in both directions — under-performance signals tightening credit conditions and growth slowdown.
indicators.so_what: Banks are sitting in the bottom quartile of relative performance vs the S&P (z = −1.06). The trend isn't deteriorating month-over-month, so the rule's level-and-trend condition isn't met — but the level alone is an under-the-surface caution.
indicators.formula: BKX/SPX = KBW Bank Index closing price / S&P 500 closing price
indicators.episodes_disclosure: Historical reference, not a forecast. Episodes are 3-month-confirmed entries into the bottom quartile of post-2006 (20y); SPX returns are price-only. Sample size is small — base-rate context only.

tiles.name: Liquidity & Policy
tiles.description_short: Adjusted Financial Conditions, real Fed funds rate, M2 YoY, term premium, Fed balance sheet trajectory.

tiles.name: Positioning & Breadth
tiles.description_short: NAAIM exposure, margin debt YoY, put/call ratio, % of S&P above 200dma, advance-decline.

data_caveats: Sprint 1 valuation distributions use the 15-year (post-2011) window for CAPE and ERP; Buffett indicator uses post-1970.
Sprint 1 credit distributions use post-1986 for IG OAS, post-2011 for HY OAS and HY/IG ratio.
Trailing P/E and leveraged-loan spread are deferred to Sprint 1.5 / Sprint 2 — both require additional data sourcing.
ISM headline used in place of ISM new-orders subindex — paid feed required for the latter.
